findings.claim: ADR-004's Decision section treats the repository module path x/auth as a live premise, naming it as the module whose account interface is being altered.
findings.consequence: If x/auth no longer exists at this path (renamed, moved, or removed), the decision's account-interface changes described here no longer map onto the current repository layout. Confirm the x/auth module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-004's Decision section treats the repository module path x/bank as a live premise, naming it as the module receiving the new balance-keeper APIs.
findings.consequence: If x/bank no longer exists at this path, the described keeper API additions (GetAllBalances, GetBalance, etc.) no longer have a home in the current tree. Confirm the x/bank module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-006's Decision section treats LevelDB as a live, currently-present software dependency underlying the existing Keybase backend being replaced.

citations.quote: the current Keybase backend based on LevelDB

findings.consequence: If LevelDB is no longer the underlying dependency (already migrated away per this ADR, or replaced again since), the premise describing the 'current' backend is stale. Confirm LevelDB is (or was) actually pinned/installed as described.

findings.claim: ADR-009's Decision section treats the repository module path x/evidence as a live premise, naming it as the module whose keeper matches and routes Evidence.

citations.quote: The `x/evidence` module's keeper is responsible for matching the `Evidence` against

findings.consequence: If x/evidence no longer exists at this path, the routing and handler mechanism described is no longer grounded in the current repository structure. Confirm the x/evidence module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-009's Decision section treats the repository module path x/slashing as a live premise, naming it as the module expected to already know the validator's consensus address.

citations.quote:
which should be known by the `x/slashing`
module

findings.consequence: If x/slashing no longer exists at this path, the evidence submission flow's dependency on slashing's existing knowledge of consensus addresses no longer holds as described. Confirm the x/slashing module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-019's Context section treats go-amino as a live, currently-utilized software dependency for binary and JSON encoding.
findings.consequence: If go-amino is no longer a dependency (fully removed post-migration), the premise that it is currently utilized is stale. Confirm go-amino is still pinned/installed as a dependency.

findings.claim: ADR-019's Decision section treats gogoprotobuf (cosmos/gogoproto) as a live software dependency that business logic should use for encoding primitive Value types.
findings.consequence: If the gogoprotobuf/gogoproto dependency is no longer present, this encoding guidance no longer has a resolvable package backing it. Confirm the gogoprotobuf/gogoproto dependency still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-019's Decision section names the concrete file path x/auth/types/codec.go as the location of the module-specific Codec interface example.
findings.consequence: If this file path no longer exists, the concrete example of a module codec interface is no longer anchored to a real file in the repository. Confirm x/auth/types/codec.go still exists.

findings.claim: ADR-019's Decision section treats the repository module path x/params as a live premise, naming it as a store still hardcoded to Amino JSON.
findings.consequence: If x/params no longer exists at this path, the described legacy Amino-JSON holdout location is no longer accurate. Confirm the x/params module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-021's Decision section names the concrete file path x/bank/types/types.proto as the location of the bank module's Query service definition.
findings.consequence: If this file path no longer exists, the example query service definition is no longer anchored to a real file in the repository. Confirm x/bank/types/types.proto still exists.

findings.claim: ADR-021's Decision section names the concrete file path x/gov/types/types.proto as the location of a hypothetical gov module query service.
findings.consequence: If this file path no longer exists, the hypothetical example is no longer anchored to a real file in the repository. Confirm x/gov/types/types.proto still exists.

findings.claim: ADR-021's Decision section names the concrete file path x/bank/module.go as the location where query service registration is implemented.
findings.consequence: If this file path no longer exists, the described RegisterQueryService implementation location is stale. Confirm x/bank/module.go still exists.

findings.claim: ADR-023's Decision section treats the repository module path x/bank as a live premise, asserting that the cosmos.bank protobuf package will continue to live at this go path.

citations.quote:
the `cosmos.bank` protobuf package will still live in
`x/bank`.

findings.consequence: If x/bank no longer exists at this path, the naming-convention guarantee described here no longer matches the repository layout. Confirm the x/bank module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-029's Decision section names the module path x/feegrant as the module being created to implement fee allowances.

citations.quote: we propose a module, `x/feegrant` which allows one account

findings.consequence: If x/feegrant does not exist at this path, the fee-grant mechanism described is not anchored to a resolvable module in the current repository. Confirm the x/feegrant module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-041's Decision section names the concrete package path x/bank/migrations/v2 as the location of the bank module's store-migration package.
findings.consequence: If this package path no longer exists, the illustrated Migrate1to2 example is no longer anchored to a real package in the repository. Confirm x/bank/migrations/v2 still exists.

findings.claim: ADR-058's Decision section names the go module path client/v2 as the new module housing the auto-generated CLI infrastructure.
findings.consequence: If client/v2 no longer exists at this path, the described CLI auto-generation infrastructure is no longer anchored to a real module. Confirm the client/v2 module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-060's Decision section treats the huandu.SkipList package as a live software dependency underlying the default app-side mempool implementation.
findings.consequence: If the huandu skiplist package is no longer a dependency, the nonceMempool implementation described no longer matches the actual dependency set. Confirm the huandu skiplist package is still pinned/installed.

findings.claim: ADR-063's Decision section treats github.com/cosmos/cosmos-sdk/runtime as a live, currently-present module path using a specific protobuf config type.

citations.quote: Currently, `github.com/cosmos/cosmos-sdk/runtime` uses the protobuf config type

findings.consequence: If this module path no longer exists or no longer uses that config type, the described current-state premise for the runtime module is stale. Confirm the module path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-063's Decision section names cosmossdk.io/core/store as the package where store services are defined.

citations.quote: Store services will be defined in the `cosmossdk.io/core/store` package.

findings.consequence: If this package path no longer exists, the described KVStoreService/MemoryStoreService/TransientStoreService definitions are not anchored to a real package. Confirm the package path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-063's Decision section names cosmossdk.io/log as the package supplying the logger via dependency injection.

citations.quote: A logger (`cosmossdk.io/log`) must be supplied using `depinject`

findings.consequence: If this package path no longer exists, the described logger-provisioning mechanism is not anchored to a real package. Confirm the package path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-063's Decision section names cosmossdk.io/core/event as the package where the event Service is defined.

citations.quote: The event `Service` will be defined in the `cosmossdk.io/core/event` package.

findings.consequence: If this package path no longer exists, the described event-emission service is not anchored to a real package. Confirm the package path still resolves.

findings.claim: ADR-064's Decision section treats the cosmosio package as a live software dependency used to marshal the canonical vote extension for signature verification.
findings.consequence: If the cosmosio package no longer exists or no longer provides MarshalDelimited, the described ValidateVoteExtensions signature-verification code is no longer accurate. Confirm the package and function still resolve.

findings.claim: ADR-070's Decision section treats the repository module path x/auth as a live premise, naming its existing KV store as the storage location for unordered-sequence replay protection.

citations.quote: we will leverage the existing x/auth KV store to store the unordered sequences.

findings.consequence: If x/auth no longer exists at this path or no longer exposes a usable KV store, the described replay-protection storage mechanism is not anchored to the current repository. Confirm the module path and its KV store still resolve.
